evals.query: My Kora service calls an external API over HTTP that needs HTTP Basic auth with a username and password. How do I add it to my @HttpClient?
expected_behavior: Registers the built-in BasicAuthHttpClientInterceptor from a @Module factory using the two-argument (username, password) constructor
Binds username and password through a @ConfigSource interface, externalizing the password with ${ENV_VAR} substitution
Attaches the interceptor with @InterceptWith(BasicAuthHttpClientInterceptor.class) on the @HttpClient interface or a method
Sets the target URL via httpClient.<client>.url in config, not a baseUrl annotation attribute
Uses constructor injection only (no field injection), no @Value-style placeholder annotation, and no non-existent interceptors = {...} attribute on @HttpClient

evals.query: The third-party API I integrate with requires an API key in the X-API-KEY header. Set this up for my Kora HTTP client.
expected_behavior: Uses the built-in ApiKeyHttpClientInterceptor with ApiKeyLocation.HEADER, the header name X-API-KEY, and the secret
Provides the key through a @ConfigSource interface referencing an environment variable
Attaches the interceptor with @InterceptWith
Mentions http-client-common (and a transport like http-client-ok) and notes there is no http-client-auth artifact
Does not use field injection or a @Value-style annotation for the key

evals.query: I have a static bearer token in an env var that I need to send on every request from my Kora HTTP client.
expected_behavior: Registers BearerAuthHttpClientInterceptor from a @Module, supplying an HttpClientTokenProvider (or the static-token constructor)
Implements HttpClientTokenProvider returning CompletionStage<String> when a provider is used
Reads the token from a @ConfigSource interface bound to ${API_TOKEN}
Attaches it with @InterceptWith(BearerAuthHttpClientInterceptor.class)
Does not invent an interceptors attribute on @HttpClient or use foreign-framework constructs

evals.query: I need to authenticate outgoing requests with a JWT fetched from an auth server, cache it, and refresh it 60 seconds before it expires.
expected_behavior: Implements HttpClientTokenProvider with getToken returning CompletionStage<String> for non-blocking fetch
Caches the token in a thread-safe component and refreshes ahead of expiry using a margin (e.g. 60s)
Fetches the token through a separate declarative @HttpClient against the token endpoint
Wires the provider into BearerAuthHttpClientInterceptor via a @Module
Does not block with .join() inside the provider as the primary approach, and does not use field injection

evals.query: Implement OAuth2 client-credentials flow for service-to-service auth in Kora: POST client_id/client_secret to the token endpoint, then send Bearer tokens to the resource server.
expected_behavior: Explains Kora has no built-in OAuth2 flow and assembles it from HttpClientTokenProvider, a token cache, and a declarative auth @HttpClient
Uses BasicAuthHttpClientInterceptor on the auth client to send client_id/client_secret and posts grant_type=client_credentials
Parses access_token and expires_in (e.g. via @Json field names) and caches with the real lifetime
Registers BearerAuthHttpClientInterceptor for the resource-server client and attaches it with @InterceptWith
Externalizes the client secret with ${OAUTH2_CLIENT_SECRET}; does not use a @Value-style annotation or field injection

evals.query: The external API wants the key in a non-standard header named X-Custom-Token. Write a custom interceptor for my Kora HTTP client.
expected_behavior: Implements HttpClientInterceptor with processRequest(Context ctx, InterceptChain chain, HttpClientRequest request) returning CompletionStage<HttpClientResponse>
Builds the authorized request with request.toBuilder().header(name, value).build() and calls chain.process(ctx, request)
Annotates the interceptor with @Component and injects config via a @ConfigSource interface in the constructor
Attaches it with @InterceptWith
Does not mutate the original request in place and does not use a @Value-style annotation

evals.query: Under load my token provider fires many simultaneous token refreshes. How do I make refresh thread-safe in Kora?
expected_behavior: Uses a synchronized block (or lock) with a double-check after acquiring it so only one refresh runs
Marks the cached token and expiry fields volatile for cross-thread visibility
Keeps getToken returning CompletionStage<String> and refreshes ahead of expiry with a margin
Recommends a single shared token cache component injected into the provider
Does not introduce foreign-framework constructs or field injection

evals.query: I added implementation "ru.tinkoff.kora:http-client-auth" and set interceptors = {MyAuth.class} on @HttpClient but the build fails and I still get 401. What is wrong?
expected_behavior: Explains that ru.tinkoff.kora:http-client-auth does not exist; depend on http-client-common plus a transport such as http-client-ok
Explains @HttpClient has no interceptors attribute and the interceptor must be attached with @InterceptWith
Confirms the auth interceptor must be a registered component (@Component or a @Module factory)
Suggests verifying the URL is configured via httpClient.<client>.url and the credential header is actually set
Does not recommend foreign-framework annotations or a @Value-style placeholder
